query: VHS scanlines TV static analog distortion CRT screen
message: sub:analog_degradation/VHS_CRT — anti_aesthetic — scanlines, TV static, color bars, pixel grids, retro CRT

response.objective_caption: A macro photograph of a digital display screen showing the repeating grid of red, green, and blue subpixels. The rectangular color elements form diagonal stripes across a black background, revealing the microscopic hardware structure that generates digital color.
response.anti_aesthetic_caption: A macro photograph of a digital display screen showing the repeating grid of red, green, and blue subpixels at an extreme, monumental magnification. The rectangular color elements form diagonal stripes across a black background, their pure red, green, and blue hues creating aggressive, toxic neon combinations that clash with one another. The unfamiliar scale disrupts ordinary perception, rendering the microscopic hardware as an abstract pattern of harsh, unbalanced color.
